title: SAO collapse remains unresolved
desc: One year after the collapse of the State Audit Office's (SAO) new headquarters in Bangkok's Chatuchak district on March 28, 2025, Thailand is still searching for answers -- not only about what caused the building to fall, but also about the deeper systemic failures the disaster exposed.

title: Diesel tax to drop by 1 baht per litre
desc: The government is preparing to reduce the excise tax on diesel by one baht per litre, aiming to ease the rising cost burden on consumers, even though the move is expected to cost the state up to 2 billion baht per month.

title: Govt prepares co-payment scheme to ease living costs
desc: The government plans to launch the "Khon La Khrueng" co-payment scheme to help ease the rising cost of living, with rollout expected after it delivers its policy statement to parliament.

title: Bangladesh seeks to build on existing ties, eyes trade deal
desc: The new government in Bangladesh is seeking to deepen its engagement with Thailand and Asean, which may include the signing of a free trade agreement (FTA) and the establishment of direct maritime connectivity, Faiyaz Murshid Kazi, Bangladesh's ambassador to Thailand, says.

title: Fuel support starts April 1
desc: The government will roll out targeted fuel subsidies for the transport sector from April 1, Transport Minister Phiphat Ratchakitprakarn has confirmed, as operators grapple with mounting costs and looming freight rate increases.

title: Bangkok Airways to raise domestic airfares by up to 20%
desc: SET-listed Bangkok Airways plans to increase domestic airfares by 15-20% from April 1 to counter rising fuel costs and a 3% decline in second-quarter forward bookings, and may negotiate with the Civil Aviation Authority of Thailand (CAAT) to extend the airfare ceiling if operating costs continue to soar.

title: Sethaput named as councillor
desc: Sethaput Suthiwartnarueput, a former governor of the Bank of Thailand (BoT), has been appointed a Privy Councillor following a royal command announced in the Royal Gazette on Thursday.

title: Thailand mulls windfall tax on oil refineries
desc: The Fiscal Policy Office (FPO) has acknowledged it is studying the feasibility of introducing a windfall tax on refinery businesses in Thailand.

title: Forum backs arbitration policy reform
desc: The Thai Arbitration Institute (THAC) under the Ministry of Justice has wrapped up its THAC Leader Forum for Public Policy on Trade, Investment and Alternative Dispute Resolution 2026.

title: An elegy to lost worlds
desc: A large, booming presence as bureau chief of the Associated Press in Bangkok for more than 30 years, a war reporter in Indochina before that, thrice president of the Foreign Correspondents Club of Thailand, Denis Gray has through six turbulent decades occupied front row seats, and foxholes, to report on some of the most violent upheavals of our times.

title: Inheriting the brush
desc: At first glance, a mural resembles a familiar backdrop to an ubosot at any temple. You expect it to depict themes from the Jataka Tales and the Tripitaka to legends and folklore. But upon closer look, it reveals something different -- it is deeply personal, vernacular and subversive of gender norms.

title: Free electric coaches ease travel costs in Chiang Rai
desc: CHIANG RAI - Chiang Rai municipality has launched a free electric coach service to help residents cope with rising fuel prices and limited supply, with the vehicles running throughout the day on routes that cover key areas of the northern city.

title: Oil checks in seven provinces find ‘no irregularities’
desc: Inspections of oil depots and petrol stations in seven provinces have found no irregularities in fuel supply or distribution, the National Economic and Social Development Council (NESDC) said on Friday.

title: Fares for Pattaya-Koh Larn ferries going up
desc: Operators of ferries between Pattaya and Koh Larn, a nearby island popular with day-trippers, say they will raise their fares from 30 baht to 40 baht next month, citing rising fuel costs that have made it difficult to sustain operations.

title: Dhammajayo case dropped as statute of limitations expires
desc: The Department of Special Investigation (DSI) has formally dropped criminal proceedings against Phra Dhammajayo, the former abbot of Wat Phra Dhammakaya, after the statute of limitations in the high‑profile money‑laundering case expired earlier this month.

title: Guru's Weekly Buzz: Mar 27-Apr 2
desc: Guru By Bangkok Post's weekly pick of the most exciting products, activities, food and travel to indulge in.

title: Pheu Thai cabinet hopeful Sudawan fails screening check
desc: Pheu Thai Party deputy leader Sudawan Wangsuphakitkosol, tipped to take one of the party’s eight cabinet seats in the Bhumjaithai-led coalition government, has failed to meet required qualifications.

title: Your horoscope for Mar 27 - April 2
desc: Your spot-on horoscope for work, money and relationship from Guru by the Bangkok Post's famously accurate fortune teller. Let's see how you will fare this week and beyond.

title: Thai factory output unexpectedly dips in February, could rise in March
desc: Thailand's manufacturing production ​index dropped 0.04% in February from a year ​earlier, the Industry Ministry ‌said on Friday, weaker than analysts' forecasts.

title: 泰国警方突击两大仓库，查获2500余架无人机
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月26日，泰国技术犯罪调查总部联合国家广播电信委员会第一区域办公室等部门，开展代号为“Crack down Drone outlaws”的联合执法行动。执法人员依据民事法院米奴府发出的搜查令，对位于曼谷的两处非法无人机销售仓库进行突击搜查，查获大量非法进口无人机。 行动中，执法人员首先对位于拉差巴里区Pra Thep Ratmori 6巷的1049/10号和1169/2号仓库展开搜查。现场发现，两处仓库规模较大，内部存放自中国进口的多种货物，包括日用品、食品及各类电子设备。执法人员共查获非法进口无人机2500余架，总价值超过500万泰铢。

title: 霍尔木兹海峡紧张，泰国及多国抢购俄罗斯原油
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据路透社报道，多方消息人士及俄方相关人士透露，受霍尔木兹海峡紧张局势影响，石油出口受阻，包括越南、泰国、菲律宾、印度尼西亚、斯里兰卡在内的多个亚洲国家近期积极寻求采购俄罗斯原油。

title: Italian drug user arrested on Kon Chang after argument with Thai wife
desc: Italian drug user arrested on Kon Chang after argument with Thai wife Legacy Koh Chang police arrested an Italian man for drug use today, March 27, after a caretaker at his rented house called officers to intervene in a violent argument between him and his Thai wife .

title: British mother seeks woman seen with son before his death in Bangkok hotel
desc: British mother seeks woman seen with son before his death in Bangkok hotel Legacy A British mother is trying to identify and contact a foreign woman who was seen with her son , Joshua Kershaw , shortly before he was found dead at a hotel in Sukhumvit area of Bangkok in January.

title: Phuket man arrested for groping 4 Thai and foreign women
desc: Phuket man arrested for groping 4 Thai and foreign women Legacy Police arrested a Thai man for sexually assaulting at least four women in the Chalong area of Phuket throughout March.

title: Tourist says they fear dogs, not humans, when walking on Thai streets
desc: A foreign tourist has shared a video comparing walking safety in California and Thailand, saying she felt less concerned about people in Thailand but stayed alert for dogs that might chase her on the street.

title: 曼谷太能打！9家餐厅杀入亚洲50最佳，两家冲进前五
desc: 国际餐饮权威榜单“2026亚洲50最佳餐厅”正式公布。泰国共有9家餐厅跻身前50强，全部位于曼谷，显示出泰国高端餐饮在亚洲范围内依然保持强劲竞争力。

title: DSI ends proceedings against ex-abbot of Phra Dhammakaya Temple
desc: The Department of Special Investigation (DSI) announced the terminated legal proceedings against the former abbot of Phra Dhammakaya Temple and the owner of the Victoria’s Secret massage parlour.

title: 瞄准芯片产业机遇，BOI赴上海吸引全球芯片企业落地泰国
desc: 泰国投资促进委员会（BOI）赴中国上海参加全球半导体行业盛会 SEMICON China 2026，向国际企业展示泰国作为区域半导体生产基地的潜力，积极吸引中国龙头企业赴泰投资。

title: Screams heard during hunt for woman carried uphill, no trace found
desc: Rescue workers and police launched a search for a missing woman at Khao Song Phi Nong in Chon Buri after a resident reported seeing two men carrying an unconscious woman uphill.

title: British and Irish riders arrested in Phuket after viral wheelie video
desc: Police arrested an Irish and a British motorcyclist after they were filmed performing wheelies on a public road in Phuket on March 25.

title: 重磅任命！泰国前央行行长获任枢密院大臣
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月26日，泰国政宪公报网站发布国王御令，正式任命前央行行长塞塔普为新任枢密院大臣，自当日起生效。 根据公告，泰国国王玛哈·哇集拉隆功陛下依据2017年版《泰王国宪法》第10条和第11条之规定，御准增补塞塔普为枢密院成员。此前枢密院大臣的任命已于2023年11月29日公告，此次为根据工作需要进行的增补。 公开资料显示，塞塔普出生于1965年，自幼随家庭在海外生活，曾在法国完成中学教育，后赴美国深造，毕业于斯沃斯莫尔学院，并在耶鲁大学获得经济学硕士和博士学位。其职业生涯起步于麦肯锡纽约分公司，随后进入世界银行担任高级经济学家。1997年亚洲金融危机期间回到泰国，参与财政政策研究工作，之后再次回到世界银行任职。 2005年后，塞塔普在泰国证券交易所、泰国商业银行及财政部等机构担任要职，并参与多家大型企业及金融机构的董事会工作，同时担任泰国央行董事及货币政策委员会委员。2020年10月起， he 出任泰国央行第24任行长，任期至2025年9月。卸任后被任命为王室财产管理机构顾问。2026年3月26日，正式获御准任命为枢密院大臣。 （编译：Juliette；审校：WAN；来源：bangkokbiznews、thairath）

title: Couple allegedly beaten by 50 men at Samut Prakan gambling den
desc: Couple allegedly beaten by 50 men at Samut Prakan gambling den Legacy A man asked the Sai Mai Must Survive page for help yesterday, March 26, alleging he and his girlfriend were assaulted by a group of more than 50 men inside an illegal gambling den in Samut Prakan , leaving them seriously injured. At 2.30pm, 33 year old Somboon filed a complaint with Ekaphop Lueangprasert, founder of the Sai Mai Must Survive page , after the alleged assault. Somboon said the incident happened on the evening of March 21 when he and his girlfriend went to play hi-lo (a dice gambling game) at the venue, which he
